pattern: Whether an element beats everything to its left, or everything to its right, only needs the running maximum from each side, so one left-to-right and one right-to-left pass answers both conditions for every position at once.
hints: An element only needs to beat one side, not both, so track the best value seen so far from the left and the best value still coming from the right separately.
Build a running maximum of everything strictly before each index, and a running maximum of everything strictly after each index.
An element is valid exactly when it is strictly greater than the running maximum from its left OR strictly greater than the running maximum from its right; collect those in their original order.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a mix of left-valid and right-valid elements
statement example, only the first and last elements qualify
two elements, both are always valid as the first and last
strictly increasing: every element beats everything to its left
strictly decreasing: every element beats everything to its right
a valley shape: the middle elements tie their neighbors' side maximum and so are not strictly greater on either side
the maximum constraint length, one hundred strictly increasing elements

stress.description: random arrays up to the full length-100 constraint with random values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
